prose:
Fukui functions for benzaldehyde (most electrophilic site f⁺ value):

Method provenance
• Property: condensed Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor) and identification of most electrophilic/nucleophilic atoms; headline number is f⁺ of the most electrophilic atom.
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF 2.4 as invoked via chemkit.
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied benzaldehyde.xyz (14 atoms) taken as-is; no geometry optimization – single-point charges on fixed geometry (fukui reactivity DFT//input).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1; cation (N–1) and anion (N+1) states treated as doublets (multiplicity 2).
• Convergence & caveats: All three SCF calculations (N, N±1) converged. Integrity check reports trustworthy: true. Density fitting was OFF (exact four-center integrals). Warnings reproduced verbatim below.

Selected results (atoms numbered as in input order)
– Most electrophilic site: atom 3 (C, index 2) with f⁺ = 0.39938968
– Most nucleophilic site: atom 1 (O, index 0) with f⁻ = 0.23839906
(The full per-atom f⁺, f⁻, and dual descriptor arrays are available in the result JSON at benzaldehyde_fukui_b3lyp_def2tzvp.json.)

Live log & files
The run was executed with:
  cli: fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 --solvent none …
A live engine log was written during the calculation; see /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpsvp69hq7/live.json for the full JSON output and `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/benzaldehyde/argo_o3/20260704-180720_benzaldehyde_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png` for the autogenerated fukui bar-chart.

• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
